Title: Configurable Product PDP Renders Configuration Widget Correctly
Preconditions: Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}. Product Bike with options (SKU: CVQ-54616437) exists at /products-with-options/configurations/bike-with-options
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurations/bike-with-options; [WAIT] Wait for Configure text to appear; [ASSERT] Observe product name at top of page; [ASSERT] Observe base product price; [ASSERT] Observe the Configure the parameters widget; [ASSERT] Observe accordion section label Select one; [ASSERT] Observe subtitle Personalize your selection further (optional); [ASSERT] Observe accordion is open (expanded) by default; [ASSERT] Verify 5 radio options visible: Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized; 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor; Seat; Pedals; None; [ASSERT] Verify None is selected by default; [ASSERT] Verify quantity stepper shows 0; [ASSERT] Verify Add to cart button is disabled when qty=0
Assertions: [DOM] Product name Bike with options visible in h1 or product title element; [DOM] Base price $350.00 displayed in price element; [DOM] Configure the parameters widget header visible; [DOM] Accordion label reads Select one with subtitle Personalize your selection further (optional); [DOM] All 5 radio options present with None selected by default; [STATE] Quantity stepper value=0; [STATE] Add to cart button has disabled attribute or aria-disabled=true at qty 0; [DOM] In stock badge visible
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors on page load; [NETWORK] Product detail GraphQL query returns 200 with configurable sections in response
Failure_Signals: Add to cart button is enabled at qty 0; price element missing or showing wrong value; widget not rendered; fewer than 5 radio options visible; None not pre-selected

Title: Selecting Radio Option Updates Total Price Immediately
Preconditions: Storefront accessible. Bike with options PDP loaded. None selected by default. Base price $350.00 visible.
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to Bike with options PDP; [WAIT] Wait for Configure widget; [ASSERT] Note base price $350.00 (None selected); [ACT] Click radio button for Seat option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $365.00; [ACT] Click radio button for Pedals option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $364.00; [ACT] Click radio button for 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $575.00; [ACT] Click radio button for Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized option; [ASSERT] Observe total price update to $526.00; [ACT] Click None radio button; [ASSERT] Observe total price returns to $350.00
Assertions: [MATH] Seat total=$365.00 ($350 base + $15 x1); [MATH] Pedals total=$364.00 ($350 base + $14 x1); [MATH] Engine total=$575.00 ($350 base + $225 x1); [MATH] Rear wheel total=$526.00 ($350 base + $88 x2=$176); [MATH] None total=$350.00; [STATE] Only one radio selectable at a time; [STATE] Price updates are immediate with no page reload; [DOM] Accordion subtitle updates to show selected option name
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors on option selection; [NETWORK] No full page reload triggered on radio click
Failure_Signals: Price does not update on radio click; multiple radios appear selected simultaneously; price update requires page reload; wrong total for any option (especially Rear wheel which is $88 x2 not x1)

Title: Each Option Row Displays Unit Price Quantity and Extended Price
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Accordion open showing all option rows.
Test_Data: url={{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurations/bike-with-options; rearwheel_unit=$88.00; rearwheel_list=$126.00; rearwheel_qty=2; rearwheel_extended=$176.00; engine_unit=$225.00; seat_unit=$15.00; pedals_unit=$14.00
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to Bike with options PDP and wait for Configure text; [ASSERT] Observe Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe Seat row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe Pedals row: read unit price quantity and extended price; [ASSERT] Observe None row: verify no price shown; [ASSERT] Verify extended price = unit price x quantity for each option
Assertions: [FORMAT] Rear wheel: unit $88.00 (salePrice) with $126.00 strikethrough (listPrice); qty 2; extended $176.00; [MATH] Engine: unit $225.00; qty 1; extended $225.00; [MATH] Seat: unit $15.00; qty 1; extended $15.00; [MATH] Pedals: unit $14.00; qty 1; extended $14.00; [DOM] None row shows no price; [MATH] Extended price = unit price x quantity for all options with prices
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No rendering errors; [NETWORK] Price data returned in product GraphQL response matches displayed values
Failure_Signals: Any option showing list price as primary price instead of sale price; extended price not matching unit x qty; None row showing a price; Rear wheel extended showing $88 instead of $176 (missed qty=2)

Title: Sale Price Shown as Primary Price with List Price Strikethrough
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Rear wheel option has salePrice $88.00 and listPrice $126.00.
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to Bike with options PDP; [WAIT] Wait for Configure widget; [ASSERT] Locate Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized option row; [ASSERT] Observe the price display on that option row; [ACT] Click the Rear wheel radio button; [ASSERT] Observe the total product price update
Assertions: [FORMAT] Rear wheel option shows $88.00 as the primary/sale price; [FORMAT] $126.00 appears with strikethrough CSS (text-decoration:line-through) as the list price; [MATH] Selecting Rear wheel: total updates to $526.00 (base $350 + extended $176 using salePrice $88 x2); [STATE] List price $126.00 is NOT used in price calculations; [FORMAT] Visual hierarchy: sale price prominent; list price secondary
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No errors; [NETWORK] GraphQL response contains both salePrice and listPrice fields for Rear wheel option
Failure_Signals: List price $126 used in total instead of sale price $88; total showing $602 ($350+$126x2) instead of $526; strikethrough not applied to list price; sale price not visually prominent; total not updating on radio click

Title: Quantity Stepper Starts at Zero and Controls Add to Cart Enable State
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Page in fresh state.
Steps: [ASSERT] Observe quantity stepper initial value=0; [ASSERT] Observe Add to cart button state at qty=0; [ACT] Attempt to click Add to cart while qty=0; [ACT] Click the + button to increment quantity to 1; [ASSERT] Observe Add to cart button becomes enabled; [ACT] Click + again to increase to 2; [ACT] Click - to decrease to 1; [ACT] Click - again to decrease to 0; [ASSERT] Observe Add to cart button disabled again; [ACT] Attempt to enter negative quantity in field if editable
Assertions: [STATE] Quantity stepper initializes at 0 on page load; [STATE] Add to cart disabled at qty=0 (greyed out or aria-disabled=true); [STATE] Clicking Add to cart at qty=0 has no effect and no error toast; [STATE] At qty=1: Add to cart button becomes enabled; [STATE] At qty=2: button remains enabled; [STATE] Decrement to 0: Add to cart disabled again; [STATE] Negative quantities not accepted

User is logged in. Configurable product with Variation section is in cart with variation Color: Red selected.
Steps: [NAV] Sign in at {{FRONT_URL}} and ensure configurable product with Variation section (Color: Red) is in cart; [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; [WAIT] Cart page loaded; [ASSERT] Locate line item with Color: Red configuration; [ACT] Click Edit or Configure button on the cart line item if available OR navigate back to PDP via item link; [WAIT] Configuration UI accessible; [ACT] Change variation selection from Color: Red to Color: Blue; [ACT] Confirm the change (Update Cart or Add to Cart); [NAV] Navigate to cart; [ASSERT] Line item reflects updated configuration
Assertions: [STATE] Variation selection updated from Color: Red to Color: Blue; [DOM] Cart line item configuration details show updated Color: Blue; [MATH] Price updated to reflect new variation salePrice; [STATE] Line item updated rather than creating a duplicate OR if duplicate: old item removed; [FORMAT] Updated configuration correctly labeled
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] updateConfigurationItem or addItem mutation reflects new variation selection; errors[] empty; [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors during edit flow; [NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx during update
Failure_Signals: Edit UI not available; configuration update creating duplicate line item with both colors; price not updating after edit; Color: Red still showing after save

Title: Checkout Preserves Variation Configuration Data

Assertions: [DOM] Checkout page shows line item with variation configuration (e.g. Color: Red); [DOM] Order confirmation page shows variation configuration data; [MATH] Order total includes variation extendedPrice; [STATE] Variation configuration data survives the cart-to-checkout transition; [NAV] URL reaches /order/confirmation after Place Order

Title: GraphQL productConfiguration Returns Sections with type Variation
Section: Configurable Products > Variation Section > GraphQL API
Preconditions: PREREQUISITE: Configurable product must have variations defined. Variation section configured with variation options via admin. Product with Variation section configuration exists in QA catalog. User authenticated with Bearer token or storefront session. GraphiQL accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql.
Steps: [AUTH] Obtain Bearer token via POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{USER_EMAIL}}/{{USER_PASSWORD}} credentials; [GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with query { productConfiguration(productId: <configurable-product-id> storeId: {{STORE_ID}}) { configurationSections { id name description type isRequired options { id quantity listPrice { amount } salePrice { amount } extendedPrice { amount } product { id name code properties { name value } } } } } }; [VAR] Extract configurationSections array from response
Assertions: [ERRORS] errors[] is empty in response; [DATA] configurationSections[] contains at least one section with type = Variation (string exact match); [DATA] Variation section options[] each contain id; quantity; salePrice.amount; extendedPrice.amount; [DATA] options[].product.properties[] contains variation-level properties (Color; Size); [SCHEMA] response.data.productConfiguration is not null; [DATA] isRequired field present as boolean
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] Query result type=Variation matches section type set via REST API POST /api/catalog/products/configurations; [CONSOLE] No errors if queried via browser GraphiQL; [ADMIN] Variation section visible in admin configurable product tab
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty despite HTTP 200; type field returning Product instead of Variation; options[] empty or missing; product.properties[] empty; extendedPrice.amount not matching salePrice x quantity

Title: addItem Mutation with Variation Section Configuration Adds to Cart

Title: Admin Creates Configuration with Variation Section Type via REST API

Admin authenticated at {{BACK_URL}}. A product exists in catalog to configure. Variation product IDs known (e.g. jeans color/size variations with known product IDs in QA catalog).

Assertions: [STATUS] POST returns HTTP 201 or 200; [BODY] Response body contains the created configuration id; [BODY] sections[].type = Variation in the response; [BODY] sections[].name = Choose Color; [BODY] sections[].isRequired = true; [BODY] sections[].options[] contains both variation product IDs; [SCHEMA] GET response contains configurationSections with the newly created Variation section
Cross_Layer_Checks: [DB] GET after POST returns the Variation section confirming persistence; [ADMIN] Navigate to {{BACK_URL}} Catalog > product > Configurable tab — Variation section visible in admin UI; [SEARCH] After 30-60s reindex; storefront productConfiguration GraphQL query returns the new Variation section
Failure_Signals: POST returning 4xx (e.g. 400 invalid type; 422 validation); type field rejected (only Product accepted); options validation failing for variation product IDs; GET after POST returning empty sections[]
Cleanup: Delete created configuration via DELETE API after test

Steps: [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurations/bike-with-options; [WAIT] Page fully loaded; [SCROLL] Scroll down past the Configure the parameters widget; [ASSERT] Locate Customers bought together section; [ASSERT] Observe related product cards in the section; [ASSERT] Verify at least one related product shows a price; [ASSERT] Verify at least one related product has an add-to-cart option
Assertions: [DOM] Customers bought together section heading visible below the configuration widget; [DOM] At least one related product card rendered in the section; [FORMAT] Related product prices shown with 2 decimal places; [DOM] Add-to-cart control (button or link) present on at least one related product card; [STATE] Section does not block or overlap the configuration widget
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors on page load; [NETWORK] Related products query returns 200 with product data; [API] GraphQL productAssociations or equivalent query returns associations for this product
Failure_Signals: Customers bought together section absent entirely; section present but empty (no product cards); related products showing without prices; section overlapping or hiding the configuration widget; console errors on related products query

Title: changeCartConfiguredItem with Invalid sectionId Returns errors[] Non-Empty
Section: Configurable Products > GraphQL xAPI > Error Handling
Preconditions: User authenticated. A configured product is in the cart. lineItemId and a valid configurationSections structure known from previous addItem.
Steps: [AUTH] Obtain Bearer token via POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{USER_EMAIL}}/{{USER_PASSWORD}}; [SETUP] Ensure a configured product is in cart — record lineItemId; [GQL] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql with mutation changeCartConfiguredItem(command: { storeId: {{STORE_ID}} userId: <user-id> lineItemId: <lineItemId> configurationSections: [{ sectionId: "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000" value: { productId: "any-id" quantity: 1 } }] quantity: 1 }) { items { configurationItems { sectionId } } errors { code description } }; [VAR] Capture errors[] array from response
Assertions: [ERRORS] errors[] is NON-EMPTY — mutation rejected invalid sectionId; [DATA] errors[].code is present and non-null; [STATE] Cart items remain unchanged (original configurationItems preserved); [DATA] HTTP status is 200 (xAPI convention) but errors[] non-empty signals rejection
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] Follow-up cart query confirms original configurationItems unchanged; [CONSOLE] No unhandled JS errors if tested via storefront; [NETWORK] HTTP 200 with non-empty errors[]
Failure_Signals: errors[] is empty despite invalid sectionId — silent acceptance of bad data; cart configurationItems changed to reflect invalid section; HTTP 4xx/5xx instead of 200+errors[]
Cleanup: No cleanup needed — mutation rejected cart unchanged

Assertions: [ERRORS] errors[] is NON-EMPTY OR configurationItems still contains required section — system either rejects empty config or preserves required selection; [STATE] Required section selection not cleared if isRequired=true; [DATA] Response does not indicate checkout-ready state with missing required section
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP] cart query confirms required configurationItem still present; [STOREFRONT] Cart page still shows required section as filled

Title: GA4 add_to_cart Event Includes Configured Total Value (Base Plus Option Surcharge)
Preconditions: Storefront accessible. User authenticated. Bike with options PDP loaded. Cart empty. dataLayer accessible.
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to Bike with options PDP; [WAIT] Configure widget visible; [ACT] Select Seat option (total $365.00); [ACT] Set qty to 1; [ACT] Click 'Add to Cart'; [WAIT] Success notification; [KEY] Execute in browser console: window.dataLayer.filter(e => e.event === 'add_to_cart'); [VAR] Capture add_to_cart event from dataLayer
Assertions: [STATE] dataLayer contains add_to_cart event after button click; [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.value = 365.00 (base $350 + Seat option $15) — NOT $350 (base only); [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.items[0].price = 365.00; [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.items[0].quantity = 1; [DATA] add_to_cart.ecommerce.currency = 'USD'
Cross_Layer_Checks: [NETWORK] GA4 collection endpoint receives add_to_cart with value=365.00; [CONSOLE] No GA4 JS errors; [API] addItem mutation returns 200 with cart item price matching 365.00
Failure_Signals: add_to_cart event missing from dataLayer; value = 350.00 (base only — option surcharge missing from analytics); value = 0; event fires before add-to-cart action completes

Title: GA4 purchase Event for Configured Product Reports Configured Total Correctly
Preconditions: User authenticated with valid payment method. Bike with options PDP accessible. Cart empty. GA4 dataLayer accessible.
Test_Data: user={{USER_EMAIL}}; password={{USER_PASSWORD}}; front_url={{FRONT_URL}}; seat_total=365.00; card={{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}}; exp={{TEST_CARD_EXP}}; cvv={{TEST_CARD_CVV}}
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to Bike with options PDP; [WAIT] Configure widget; [ACT] Select Seat option (total $365.00); [ACT] Set qty 1; [ACT] Click 'Add to Cart'; [WAIT] Success notification; [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; [ACT] Proceed to checkout — complete shipping address and payment with {{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}}/{{TEST_CARD_EXP}}/{{TEST_CARD_CVV}}; [ACT] Click 'Place Order'; [WAIT] Order confirmation page loads; [KEY] Execute in browser console: window.dataLayer.filter(e => e.event === 'purchase'); [VAR] Capture purchase event from dataLayer
Assertions: [STATE] dataLayer contains purchase event after order confirmation page loads; [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.value = 365.00 (configured total including option — NOT just base $350); [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.transaction_id matches order number on confirmation page; [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.items[0].price = 365.00; [DATA] purchase.ecommerce.items[0].quantity = 1; [DATA] purchase event fires exactly once (not on confirmation page refresh)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [NETWORK] GA4 collection endpoint receives purchase payload; [CONSOLE] No GA4 JS errors; [ADMIN] Order exists in admin with matching total; [API] Order total = 365.00 + shipping + tax
Failure_Signals: purchase event missing; value = 350.00 (base only — option surcharge lost in analytics); transaction_id null or mismatched; purchase fires twice on confirmation page refresh
Cleanup: Order placed — cancel via admin if needed for re-run

Title: E2E: Eventual Consistency — Config Section Price Change Reflected on Storefront Within 120 Seconds
Preconditions: Admin logged in at {{BACK_URL}}. Configurable Hat product exists with a Product section option. Storefront PDP for Configurable Hat accessible. Initial option price known.
Steps: [NAV] In admin: navigate to {{BACK_URL}} > Catalog > Products > Configurable Hat > Configuration tab; [ASSERT] Record current price of the first Product section option (initial_option_price); [ACT] Edit the option price to initial_option_price + $5.00; [SAVE] Save the configuration change; [WAIT] Note exact timestamp T0 of save; [NAV] Immediately navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/configurable-hat in storefront; [ASSERT] Check if option price already updated — may still show old price within first 60s (acceptable per BL-CAT-003); [WAIT] 120 seconds from T0; [NAV] Hard reload the storefront PDP; [ACT] Select the edited option; [ASSERT] Observe total price
Assertions: [STATE] Within first 60s: old price may still be shown on storefront (acceptable — within lag window per BL-CAT-003); [STATE] At T0+120s: storefront MUST show new updated option price — old price after 120s is a BL-CROSS-009 violation; [MATH] Total price after option selection = base price + new_option_price (initial + $5.00); [FORMAT] Price formatted to 2 decimal places
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] productConfiguration GraphQL query at T0+120s returns updated salePrice for the edited option; [ADMIN] Configuration blade still shows new option price (admin always live); [CONSOLE] No errors on price display
Failure_Signals: Storefront still shows old option price after 120s; total price calculation uses stale price; productConfiguration GraphQL response cached beyond 120s; admin save fails silently
Cleanup: Restore option price to initial value after test
